title: Shri Bilvashtakam
intro: Read Shri Bilvashtakam with Hindi meaning. Chant this sacred hymn to seek the blessings of Lord Shiva.
description:
|| Shri Bilvashtakam ||

1. Tridalam trigunakaram trinetram cha triyayudham.
Trijanma papasamharam ekabilvam shivarpanam.||
2. Trishakhaih bilvapatraishcha achchidraih komalaih shubhaih.
Tavapujam karishyami ekabilvam shivarpanam.||
3. Koti kanya mahadanam tilaparvata kotayah.
Kanchanam kshiradanena ekabilvam shivarpanam.||
4. Kashikshetra nivasa cha Kalabhairava darshanam.
Prayage Madhavam drishtva ekabilvam shivarpanam.||
5. Induvare vratam sthitva niraharo maheshvarah.
Naktam haushyami devesha ekabilvam shivarpanam.||
6. Ramalinga pratishtha cha vaivahika kritam tatha.
Tatakanicha sandhanam ekabilvam shivarpanam.||
7. Akhanda bilvapatram cha ayutam shivapujanam.
Kritam nama sahasrena ekabilvam shivarpanam.||
8. Umaya sahadavesha nandi vahanameva cha.
Bhasmalepana sarvangam ekabilvam shivarpanam.||
9. Salagrameshu vipranam tatakam dashakupayoh.
Yajnakoti sahasrascha ekabilvam shivarpanam.||
10. Danti koti sahasreshu ashvamedha shatakratau.
Kotikanya mahadanam ekabilvam shivarpanam.||
11. Bilvanam darshanam punyam sparshanam papanashanam.
Aghora papasamharam ekabilvam shivarpanam.||
12. Sahasraveda pateshu brahmastapana muchyate.
Anekavrata kotinam ekabilvam shivarpanam.||
13. Annadana sahasreshu sahasropa nayanam tatha.
Aneka janmapapani ekabilvam shivarpanam.||

Phalashruti:
14. Bilvastotramidam punyam yah pathesh-shiva sannidhau.
Shivalokam avapnoti ekabilvam shivarpanam.||

|| Iti Shri Bilvashtakam Sampurnam ||

faq.a1: It is a sacred hymn dedicated to Lord Shiva, which highlights the spiritual significance of offering the Bilva leaf (Bel patra) to the deity.
faq.q2: Why is the Bilva leaf significant?
faq.a2: The three leaves of the Bilva are considered a symbol of Lord Shiva's three eyes (Trinetra) and the Hindu Trinity (Brahma, Vishnu, and Mahesh).
faq.q3: What is the best time to recite it?
faq.a3: Reciting it on Mondays, during Pradosh Vrat, or on Mahashivaratri is considered highly auspicious, though it can be recited daily for spiritual benefit.
faq.q4: What are the benefits of reciting this stotram?
faq.a4: Recitation is believed to cleanse the sins of many lifetimes and helps the devotee attain the abode of Lord Shiva (Shivaloka), bringing inner peace and spiritual merit.
